title: The Great Pyramids at Giza
topic_path: Predynastic, Early Dynastic, and Old Kingdom

assessment_items.question_content:
Which Egyptian kings built the great pyramids at Giza?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Tutankhamun ruled during the New Kingdom of Egypt and lived thousands of years after the pyramids at Giza were built.
Djoser was buried in an early type of Egyptian pyramid characterized by its stepped platforms at Saqqara, not Giza.

The great pyramids at Giza were built by the kings Khufu, Khafre, and Menkaure.

**[Read this to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-egypt/a/old-kingdom-the-great-pyramids-of-giza)**

assessment_items.question_content: **Associated with the pyramid of [[☃ dropdown 1]], the Great Sphinx represents the head of the Egyptian king on the body of a  [[☃ dropdown 2]]**
hints:
A part of Khafre's pyramid complex, *The Great Sphinx* is a colossal depiction of a recumbent lion with the head of a king.  It sits next to the causeway leading from Khafre’s valley temple to the mortuary temple.

**[Read this to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-egypt/a/old-kingdom-pyramid-of-khafre-and-the-great-sphinx)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is NOT found at Khufu's mortuary complex?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Only Khafre's retains any of the limestone casing used for the great pyramids.

**[Read this to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-egypt/a/old-kingdom-pyramid-of-khufu)**

choices.content: portions of the pyramid's limestone casing
a large inner chamber called the Grand Gallery
a burial chamber made of red granite
several large boat pits to transport the king in the afterlife

assessment_items.question_content:
**The shape of the pyramids is probably a reference to  $\_\_\_\_\_\_$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The shape of the pyramid was probably a solar reference.

**[Read this to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-egypt/a/old-kingdom-the-great-pyramids-of-giza)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which part of Khufu's mortuary complex was located nearest the floodplain?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Great Sphinx is part of the funerary complex of Khafre.

Each pyramid was part of a royal mortuary complex that also included a temple at its base and a long stone causeway to a valley temple on the edge of the floodplain.

[Read this to review.](/test-prep/ap-art-history/ancient-mediterranean-AP/ancient-egypt-AP/a/old-kingdom-the-great-pyramids-of-giza)

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to the text, the capstone, or pyramidion, of Khufu's pyramid was probably $\_\_\_\_\_\_$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
At the very top of the pyramid would have sat a capstone, known as a pyramidion, that may have been gilt.

[Read this to review.](/test-prep/ap-art-history/ancient-mediterranean-AP/ancient-egypt-AP/a/old-kingdom-pyramid-of-khufu)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What cultural values of ancient Egypt are demonstrated by the pyramids of Giza?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The three primary pyramids on the Giza plateau were built over the span of three generations by the rulers Khufu, Khafre, and Menkaure.
The pyramids provided the burial chamber for the king and were one part of the ruler's funerary complex, used for religious rites associated with the afterlife.
Like most Egyptian art, the pyramids reveal the clear geometry and mathematical precision characteristic of this ancient culture.

All of the answers above constitute important cultural values in ancient Egypt.

**[Read this to review.](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-egypt/a/old-kingdom-the-great-pyramids-of-giza)**

choices.content: The importance of ritual and tradition
The connection of kings to the gods
